title: Reboulea obtusata

taxonomy: Reboulea obtusata belongs to the family Asclepiadaceae, which is now commonly classified under the family Apocynaceae. This classification is based on the APG III system of plant classification. Reboulea obtusata is a genus of flowering plants, specifically a type of milkweed vine. The plant is native to tropical regions and is known for its medicinal properties. Its classification under Apocynaceae reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in this family.

side_effects: Reboulea obtusata, also known as the climbing snail vine, has been reported to cause skin irritation in some individuals due to its sap. Contact with the sap may lead to redness, itching, and rashes. While generally not toxic, ingesting the plant can cause gastrointestinal issues such as nausea and vomiting. More research is needed to fully understand the side effects of Reboulea obtusata, especially long-term exposure.
